items.title: A/B test harness changes on live traffic, then keep what users actually keep
items.why: Offline scores miss whether agent output truly lands cleanly in a developer workflow; keep-rate and satisfaction signals show whether a harness change reduced rework or just looked better in a benchmark.
how_to_steps: Deploy two harness variants side by side on real traffic instead of promoting changes from offline scores alone.
Track keep rate for agent-authored code after fixed intervals so you can see which output survives without manual cleanup.
Read follow-up user messages with a rubric or judge model to separate genuinely helpful output from output that only looked correct.
Alert on unknown tool-call errors immediately and baseline expected errors per tool and per model so regressions surface fast.
items.evidence: Cursor says a more expensive context-summarization variant made a negligible quality difference that was not worth the added cost, and that a focused sprint using these signals drove unexpected tool-call errors down by an order of magnitude.
items.try_today: Take one harness tweak you were about to ship on intuition alone, run it against a control for a day, and compare keep rate plus tool-error anomalies before you flip it on by default.
